payload.message: A systems design view of KV Cache Decode Latency is that cache reuse only helps when residency, paging, and scheduling all line up with the access pattern of decode. The model avoids recomputing historical attention state, but the serving stack still has to keep those tensors in the right memory layout and batch shape. Once traffic gets bursty, cache movement and fragmentation can erase some of the theoretical win. That is why strong implementations treat KV cache as a serving architecture problem instead of a purely algorithmic optimization.
